metadata.title: The Fourier Transform: Winding a Signal Around a Circle

beats.narration_text: Ciao. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of the fourier transform: winding a signal around a circle. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of the fourier transform: winding a signal around a circle
output: ✓ The Fourier Transform: Winding a Signal Around a Circle

beats.narration_text: The Fourier Transform: Winding a Signal Around a Circle is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Wind a signal around a circle — at the signal's frequency, the center of mass lurches to one side. That lurch IS the Fourier transform.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: The Fourier Transform: Winding a Signal Around a Circle

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of The Fourier Transform: Winding a Signal Around a Circle. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Signal. Verify: Wind a signal around a circle — at the signal's frequency, the center of mass lurches to one side. That lurch IS the Fou. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: the fourier transform: winding a signal around a circle. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Signal from 0 to 6. Watch the physics respond in real time.

beats.narration_text: Wind a signal around a circle — at the signal's frequency, the center of mass lurches to one side. That lurch IS the Fourier transform.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of the fourier transform: winding a signal around a circle. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this The Fourier Transform: Winding a Signal Around a Circle simulation and add a second slider that controls Signal. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
